item.title: Macau transforms Sino-Lusophone fair into an event for Portuguese-speaking countries
item.description: The Government announced yesterday that it will organize a trade fair in October dedicated only to Portuguese-speaking countries, instead of the usual event that brings together companies from China and Portuguese-speaking markets. The Macau Trade and Investment Promotion Institute (IPIM) launched a competition to coordinate the Exhibition of Products and Services of Portuguese Speaking Countries (Macau) 2026 (PLPEX), scheduled for between 21 and 24 October. The specifications foresee that PLPEX will occupy an area of ​​six thousand square meters, with around 200 stands for exhibitors “in line with Macau's positioning as a Sino-Lusophone platform”. The fair aims to attract at least 200 exhibitors, 10% of which must be local companies, 10% from Spain and 80% from Portuguese-speaking markets, “with it being mandatory to attract the participation of companies from all nine Portuguese-speaking countries”. The agricultural sector must represent at least 30% of all exhibitors, cross-border e-commerce 20% and the blue economy, linked to the sea, at least 10%, stated in the specifications. The PLPEX organizer must also create a campaign to promote the fair in Portugal and Spain. The second edition of C-PLPEX “attracted 500 exhibitors, including 320 Portuguese speakers, an increase of 23% compared to the first edition”, said, before the start of the fair, the president of IPIM. “A third are from Brazil, a third from Portugal and the rest are from [other] Portuguese-speaking countries”, added Che Weng Keong. The inaugural edition of C-PLPEX took place in 2023, but was not held in 2024. In October 2024, the then president of IPIM, Vincent U U Sang, did not confirm whether the event would become biennial. "We still have to discuss with the other organizers. If we have good results [in 2025], we will evaluate it," Vincent U told Lusa. Without C-PLPEX, the 2024 edition of MIF had 183 exhibitors from nine Portuguese-speaking countries, a reduction compared to 2023, when around 260 Portuguese-speaking exhibitors participated. The current leader of the Government of Macau, Sam Hou Fai, also highlighted the promotion of financial and commercial services between China and Hispanic countries as a priority.

item.title: Macau must make an effort to better welcome foreign tourists, say deputies
item.description:
Ho Ion Sang and Chan Lai Kei presented written requests to the Government in which they denounced several points for improvement in the reception of international tourists. Some of the measures proposed by deputies include the training of multilingual tourist guides and a more diverse offer of electronic payment options.
Macau must optimize its capacity to welcome international tourists, strengthening aspects such as service in foreign languages ​​and the variety of electronic payment services. This was the position defended by two deputies to the Legislative Assembly (AL), Ho Ion Sang and Chan Lai Kei, in their respective written statements to the Government. In his interpellation, Ho Ion Sang notes that the “trend of continuous increase in international tourists” arriving in the city has been accompanied by the implementation of several measures, such as “Check ‘N Fly” services (in which passengers can complete flight check-in and drop off their luggage at designated hotels) and “Fly You to Macau” (in which tourists entering Hong Kong through the airport have free bus tickets to Macau). Still, he recognizes that some aspects can be improved. It mentions, in a first point, that free bus travel from Hong Kong to Macau could also be extended to boat travel, in order to increase “the flexibility of itineraries” and promote “complementarity between road and maritime services”. On a second point, the leader of the Residents argues that the diversity of international tourists in the region requires a greater “general reception capacity, especially with regard to services in foreign languages”. It is recalled that the total number of international tourists in Macau was 2,755,474 in 2025, an increase of 13.7% compared to the same period last year. In the list of the ten most represented tourist markets in Macau, the highlights are South Korea (with an annual increase of 11.3%), the Philippines (9.5% more), Indonesia (3.9% more), Thailand (38.1% more), the United States of America (9.8% more) and Japan (26.1% more). Ho Ion Sang recalls that, last year, the Macau Tourism University (UTM) sought to launch training courses for guides in “less common languages”, most of which were never taught “due to an insufficient number of enrollees”. The deputy considers that the response to this weak demand could involve offering “more flexible models of language training for tourist guides”, such as mixed face-to-face and online teaching, which could contribute to increasing “the attractiveness of registration and training in this area”. Finally, Ho Ion Sang also suggests adopting the use of technological tools as a response to the shortage of human resources. According to this suggestion, tour guides and employees who work in the customer service area and do not speak foreign languages ​​could, for example, use artificial intelligence systems or real-time translation devices, in order to communicate effectively with international visitors and provide them with “a satisfactory tourist experience”.
REINFORCE THE CONNECTION TO COMMUNITY AREAS
Deputy Chan Lai Kei also sent the Government a written statement in which he drew attention to the aspects that needed to be improved in terms of the experience of international tourists, particularly with regard to consumption and travel. “The Lunar New Year is not only a peak period for tourists from the interior of China to visit Macau, but also a golden moment to show the image of Macau as a city of convergence between East and West and cultural exchange”, highlights the deputy of the Macau Institution People's Alliance, a list linked to the Fujian community. In fact, data from the Tourism Services Bureau (DST) indicates that Macau welcomed a total of 1,554,285 visitors during the golden week of the New Year of the Horse, of which 78% were from the interior of China. Chan Lai Kei considers that the main obstacles for international visitors are related to “insufficient payment methods”, which should be expanded to cover more electronic wallets, and “language services”, which would benefit from the training of multilingual tour guides. “What is the current situation (…) in terms of optimizing the experience of international tourists, which aims to effectively eliminate these tourists’ problematic points during consumption and their travel in Macau?”, he asks. In his interpellation, Chan Lai Kei also identifies as problematic aspects the concentration of tourists in traditional tourist attractions, such as the Ruins of São Paulo, and the negative consequences for small and medium-sized companies in community areas. A solution proposed by the deputy involves “implementing exclusive incentive plans for consumption in community areas and connecting transport services, with a view to extending the flow of passengers” to the less visited areas of the city. In the same message, the significant increase in day-trippers in Macau is also highlighted. “The mere increase in the number of tourists can no longer reflect real tourist efficiency”, points out the deputy, asking: “How is the Government going to analyze, in a scientific way, the structure of tourist expenses, (…) in order to attract more tourists to stay another night or to enter community neighborhoods?

item.title: Shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz causes delays in the transport of Portuguese products
item.description:
The blockage of the Strait of Hormuz, crucial for maritime oil transport, could cause delays of more than three months on routes between Europe and Asia. According to Sunny Ip, president of the Macau Suppliers Union Association, current geopolitical challenges could affect the transport and import of Portuguese products such as wine, olive oil and sardines.
The conflict in the Middle East is impacting the maritime transport of goods between Europe and Asia, with possible consequences for the prices of some products of European origin sold in Macau. According to the newspaper Ou Mun, the recent blockage of the Strait of Hormuz and restrictions on commercial navigation in the Red Sea are having an impact on the “persistent increase” in the duration and cost of maritime transport between both continents. The prolongation of the conflict could also result in an increase in the prices of European imports. Sunny Ip, president of the Macau Suppliers Union Association, notes that previous incidents in the Red Sea had already prolonged Eurasian maritime transport “from one month to more than two months”, affecting Portuguese products such as wine, olive oil and sardines. After Iran announced this Monday the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the most important and strategic maritime routes in the world, transit time could increase “to three months or more”, says the official – which, in turn, will contribute to increasing the costs of storing and handling goods. The increase in the price of Brent oil, which has already risen by more than 12% in just three days, is also increasing transportation costs. According to the same source, tariffs for transporting a single container between Europe and Asia have already reached more than 10,000 renminbi. On the other hand, the exchange rates of the countries involved are currently in a phase of high volatility, causing the financial market to operate under greater tension. Sonny Ip states that goods originating in Europe and Africa “represent only 10% to 20% of Macau's total imports”, with emphasis on luxury products and dairy products. The reserves in the inventory of goods guarantee, for now, the stability of supply for another “two to three months”, at the same time that the diversification of supply sources – with the increasing import of dairy products from the interior of China, Australia and New Zealand – reduces dependence on the European market. In turn, the logistics chain for mass market products should not be affected by the conflict in the Middle East, as these goods mostly originate from mainland China and Southeast Asia. The United States and Israel launched a military attack against Iran last Saturday, which the American president justified with the aim of “eliminating imminent threats from the Iranian regime”. Iran responded by launching missiles and drones against Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Israel. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced this Monday the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which had already been partially closed in February, with consequences for the price of oil and goods and services around the world.

item.title: Wynn Palace brings together bars from six world cocktail capitals
item.description:
Wynn Palace will once again host, on the 13th and 14th of March, the second edition of Wing Lei Bar & Friends, an event that brings together some of the most acclaimed bars in the world in Macau. With the participation of six renowned venues from New York, London, Bangkok, Singapore, New Delhi and Jakarta, the initiative promises two days of immersion in global cocktail culture, through guest shifts, debates and cultural exchanges.
After the success of the inaugural edition, Wing Lei Bar & Friends returns for its second chapter. Scheduled for the 13th and 14th of March, Volume 2 of this event, which takes place over four quarterly editions, brings to Macau bars from six cities and their respective mixologists with a strong influence on the world panorama of conctail creation, namely from the cities of New York, London, Bangkok, Singapore, New Delhi and Jakarta. Under the theme “Liquid Culture: How Global Hotspots are Shaking Up the Cocktail World”, this edition once again makes Macau the meeting point between consolidated cocktail capitals and emerging centers of innovation in the area. The objective, as the organization describes, is to create a dynamic dialogue between traditions and classics, with different techniques and sensibilities, in a format that combines counter service, discussions and professional exchange between the public and the masters present. The program starts next Friday, March 13th, with a presentation of the UNITY cocktail menu, a signature of Wing Lei Bar. This will be followed by a night that will feature the participation of renowned venues such as Opium, from Bangkok, considered the 43rd best bar in Asia in 2025 by the Asia’s 50 Best Bars list. Next will come Night Hawk, from Singapore, who holds the 77th position in the aforementioned ranking, and their compatriots the Native, who are in 45th position in the ranking. On the same night, the New York team Sip & Guzzle, 39th on The World’s 50 Best Bars 2025 list, closes its roster. On Saturday, March 14th, work begins with a panel discussion entitled “The Death or Rebirth of the Classic Cocktail”, moderated by Mark Lloyd, representing Wing Lei Bar, which will bring together some of the participating bartenders. The afternoon and evening will be hosted by No Vacancy, from New Delhi, and Modernhaus, from Jakarta, the 12th best bar in Asia according to the 2025 ranking. To conclude, the British Archive & Myth appear, in 18th position in the ranking of the best bars in the United Kingdom in 2026. Wing Lei Bar & Friends was conceived as a genuine dialogue platform for the industry, going beyond the mere concept of “guest shifts”. The first edition demonstrated the appetite for this type of exchange, attracting more than a hundred bartenders, critics and enthusiasts from across Asia for a weekend of collaborations and participatory experiences. This second edition deepens this premise by bringing together cities that have shaped global cocktail trends alongside others that are quickly emerging as new centers of innovation. The result is less of a competition and more of a conversation that takes place over the counter, at the panel table and in informal moments between clients and professionals, assures the team organizing the event at Wynn Palace. The spirit of connection between participants also extends to the cocktails served. The collectible sticker campaign, a recognized brand from the previous event, returns in a more evolved version. For each collaborative cocktail ordered, visitors receive limited edition “blind” packages, encouraging exchange and organic interaction between those present. What starts as a playful gesture will become a social catalyst, leading strangers to share stories as naturally as they exchange stickers.

item.title: CPPCC highlights national integration work of Macau and Hong Kong
item.description:
The annual session of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC) kicked off yesterday in Beijing with a positive assessment of the work on the national integration of Macau and Hong Kong over the past year. Wang Huning, president of this main political consultative body in China, said that there has been a strengthening of the articulation of rules and mechanisms in the Greater Bay Area.
The work carried out to support the “better integration and service” of Macau and Hong Kong in national development was one of the highlights of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC) assessment of last year. Wang Huning, chairman of China's main political consultative body, yesterday presented the respective work report at the opening of the fourth session of the 14th CPPCC, at the Great Hall of the People, in Beijing, where he was attended by Chinese President Xi Jinping, together with other members of the leadership of the Chinese Communist Party. Summarizing last year's activities, Wang Huning highlighted that he had organized inspections and study visits for CPPCC members, with the aim of “supporting Hong Kong and Macau to integrate better and serve the national development situation”. According to the politician, 66 members from Hong Kong and Macau carried out 107 awareness-raising activities on the national situation on campuses last year, in addition to organizing the eighth “Two Straits Grassroots Governance Forum”. “242 members from Hong Kong and Macau and Chinese representatives abroad were also invited to participate in the commemoration of the 80th anniversary of the victory in the Chinese People's War of Resistance against Japanese Aggression and the World Anti-Fascist War, and to attend the military parade”, he added. Wang Huning also highlighted the tasks carried out to strengthen the articulation of rules and mechanisms in the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area, as well as “guiding Chinese communities abroad to effectively convey China's voice”.
“INSPIRED” MACAU MEMBERS
After attending the inaugural session of the CPPCC, Macau members said they felt “encouraged”, assuring that they will take advantage of their role to promote the territory's development. Alvis Lo, also director of Health Services, committed to presenting more proposals related to the medical and health sector, contributing to the national development of the Greater Bay Area and Macau in the area of ​​health. Speaking to Chinese-language Radio Macau, deputy to the Legislative Assembly Wong Kit Cheng indicated that she was “motivated” by the recognition of the work of CPPCC members, saying that she will work to “transmit well the spirit of the ‘Two Sessions’ in Macau. Lam Lon Wai, also a deputy, said he is paying attention to the integration of social security systems, such as pension and social security schemes, in the Greater Bay Area, expressing the hope that there will be more measures that improve the well-being and safety of residents. O 15.º Plano Quinquenal Nacional, que será discutido nas “Duas Sessões” da Assembleia Popular Nacional (APN) e da CCPPC deste ano, é também foco de trabalho para os membros de Macau. Lee Chong Cheng, presidente da direcção da Federação das Associações dos Operários de Macau, afirmou ao Jornal Ou Mun que vai envidar esforços para promover o desenvolvimento nacional e garantir a implementação do 15.º Plano Quinquenal, estando concentrado em acelerar a integração nacional de Macau e aumentar o sentimento de felicidade da população.

item.title: Studio City restaurant and bar debut spring menus
item.description: The DIVA restaurant and the Blind Tiger bar, both located in Studio City's Hotel W, launched new seasonal menus yesterday to mark the arrival of Spring. In a statement, Melco details that both spaces are guided by a concept in which “time-honored traditions” are reinvented and served with “vibrant flavors and refined craftsmanship”. DIVA's new menu is presented by chef Kwok-Hung Cheng, who has used “precise Cantonese techniques” and ingredients “at the peak of ripeness” to create a dining experience inspired by the spring season. Among the new dishes, the combination of giant clam with Matsumoto mushrooms stands out; crispy duck with fresh pepper and chives; lobster with Haudiao rice wine; fish fin soup with whelk, chrysanthemum and goji; and Angus beef ribs with hot and sour soup. DIVA customers will also be able to enjoy a special limited edition menu, with prices starting from 1,888 patacas, featuring Alaskan crab as the protagonist. In one version, the crab is steamed with Haudiao rice wine and egg white, providing “a rich, tender flavor”; in another version, it is fried in a wok with garlic and red pepper, which give it a “strong and aromatic flavor”. In turn, the Blind Tiger bar presents a new cocktail menu, entitled “New Spin”, in which classics are served with a contemporary perspective. “Smoked Rusty Nail” brings a wave of heat and depth with the combination of Scottish whiskey and Drambuie liqueur. The “Salted Whiskey Sour” combines bourbon and fresh lemon with salted plum, in a combination of citrus and savory flavors. As the name suggests, “Aged Old Fashion” was aged in oak for two months, while “God Father” lives up to the strength of its name with the fusion of Sazerac Rye and Amaretto Disaronno. In total, there are nine modern interpretations of the classics that “honor tradition” and, at the same time, give “an additional dimension to time-honored recipes”. DIVA is open every day, from noon to 3pm and from 6pm to 10:10pm. Blind Tiger is open from 3pm to midnight, from Sunday to Thursday, and from 3pm to 1am, on Fridays and Saturdays.

item.title: Real estate rents in Macau are expected to stabilize in 2026
item.description: Real estate consultancy JLL points out that housing and commercial space rents in Macau are expected to stabilize this year, even with property values ​​still under pressure. In a report, the company stated that residential prices, which fell sharply in 2025, are expected to remain stable in 2026 A market forecast released yesterday by real estate consultancy JLL indicates that rents for housing and commercial spaces in Macau are expected to stabilize this year, even with asset values ​​still under pressure. In a report, the company said residential prices, which fell sharply in 2025, are expected to remain stable in 2026 following government measures to ease mortgage burdens, including stamp duty exemptions and easing loan-to-property value ratios. Banks also cut interest rates late last year, helping to sustain demand. “Property developers reduced prices to boost sales last year and, with supportive policies now in place, the residential market is expected to stabilize in the short term,” said JLL Senior Director of Valuation and Risk in Macau, Mark Wong. “But limited long-term demand and the absence of major infrastructure projects will continue to weigh on the sector,” he added, according to the report. The data shows that the equity value of housing fell by 16.5% in 2025, while that of luxury properties fell by 14.7%. Rents for conventional units fell by 10.3%, although rents for high-end residences rose by 1.1%. JLL expects both income and asset values ​​to remain stable in 2026. The office market also faced difficulties last year, with general rents falling 3.4% and asset values ​​falling 7.9%. The unemployment rate rose to 15.3%, reflecting weak demand. JLL – the report also reads – expects rents to stabilize in 2026, although asset value could fall by up to 5%. The retail sector also experienced pressure, with total sales falling 5.4% between January and September last year. Rents fell by 0.9% in 2025. Although tourist areas are beginning to attract investors again, neighborhood stores remain under pressure due to high loan default rates. JLL predicts that retail asset values ​​could fall by another 5% this year. Macau's economy showed signs of resilience in 2025, with gross domestic product (GDP) growing 4.7% to 415.32 billion patacas and gaming revenue rising 9.1% to 247.4 billion patacas. Gaming revenue from the VIP segment increased by 24.1% to MOP67.98 billion. Visitor arrivals grew by 14.7% last year, to 40 million, driven mainly by tourists from mainland China. Despite the recovery in tourism and gaming, Wong warned of persistent structural challenges. “Economic growth has returned to pre-pandemic levels, but the drivers are concentrated in just a few industries,” he said. “Sentiment in the commercial market is weak and the closure of satellite casinos will continue to pose challenges,” he said. Lusa

item.title: Collective case of gastroenteritis infected 13 users and workers at the Bethany Asylum
item.description: Os Serviços de Saúde registaram um caso de infecção colectiva de gastroenterite no Asilo de Betânia, na Avenida do Conselheiro Borja, tendo sido infectados 13 utentes e trabalhadores. Authorities say that patients began to show symptoms such as vomiting and abdominal pain, with the majority undergoing treatment in health institutions. There were no records of serious cases or other serious complications. The possibility of alimentary gastroenteritis was excluded according to patients' meal times. According to the times of occurrence of the disease, the symptoms, the incubation period, it is likely that the pathogen is related to a viral infection. A detailed investigation and monitoring of the case is now being carried out, guiding the aforementioned establishment regarding control and infection measures, in particular the correct way to treat vomiting and excrement, as well as maintaining good air ventilation inside the facilities, indicate the Health Services. The Health Services explain that norovirus and rotavirus are common viral gastroenteritis diseases, spreading very easily. Norovirus infection is easy to occur in public facilities, particularly in nursing homes and schools, as well as among various age groups. The transmission route includes the consumption of food or water possibly contaminated by this virus; contact with vomit or waste from sick people; contact with contaminated objects; or transmission by droplets of saliva. The incubation of the infection normally occurs between 24 and 48 hours. Both contagious diseases have identical symptoms, namely nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain and mild fever. In general, the symptoms are mild, and these are self-limiting illnesses, lasting one to five days, without complications. The two viruses need to be confirmed by laboratory analysis.

item.title: China begins main annual political meeting with opening of consultative conference
item.description:
China yesterday began its main annual political meeting with the opening of the session of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), the consultative body that precedes the inauguration, this Thursday, of the National People's Assembly, a legislative body.
Chinese President Xi Jinping participated in the inaugural session held at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, accompanied by other members of the leadership of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). During the meeting, the work on the agenda was approved and Wang Huning, considered one of the regime's main ideologues and close to Xi, presented the activity report of the consultative body's standing committee. The opening of the so-called “Two Sessions”, China's biggest annual political event, takes place at the beginning of the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030), which will define the country's economic and strategic priorities for the next five years. Today, Prime Minister Li Qiang is expected to announce the economic growth target for 2026, which, according to analysts, could be between 4.5% and 5%. The annual meeting also takes place after the expulsion, in recent weeks, of several senior military officials from both the National Popular Assembly and the consultative body itself, as part of the anti-corruption campaign that has reduced the leadership of the armed forces. The Defense budget, which in last year's session increased by 7.2% year-on-year, and political messages aimed at the Armed Forces should also focus part of the attention during the debates. The session is also taking place in a context of growing international tensions, following attacks by the United States and Israel against Iran, and before a planned visit by US President Donald Trump to China, which has not yet been officially confirmed by Beijing. In the first contacts with the local press, some members of the consultative body highlighted topics such as industrial and technological modernization. The president of car manufacturer Geely, Li Shufu, called, in statements cited by the newspaper The Paper, to avoid “senseless competition” in the electric vehicle sector, while other delegates highlighted advances in quantum technology and the need to accelerate the regulation of autonomous vehicles and humanoid robotics. During the session of the legislative body, Foreign Minister Wang Yi is expected to hold his usual annual press conference, an event often used to anticipate the tone of Chinese diplomacy in an increasingly volatile international context.
“Europe needs China and China needs Europe”
The spokesperson for the National People's Congress of China, Lou Qinjian, stated yesterday that “Europe needs China and China needs Europe”, defending the complementary nature of the relationship between both parties, in the face of growing trade tensions. During a press conference held on the eve of the annual session of China's legislative body, which begins this Thursday, Lou recalled that last year marked the 50th anniversary of the establishment of diplomatic relations between China and the European Union and highlighted that high-level contacts were “frequent”. According to him, the economic and commercial relationship between Beijing and Brussels is based on “complementarity of advantages and mutual benefit”, adding that cooperation with China contributes to European economic development and the improvement of social well-being on the continent. Lou also stated that between the two parties “there are no fundamental conflicts of interest or geopolitical contradictions” and expressed Beijing’s willingness to “adequately manage commercial differences”. The spokesperson also highlighted that, last year, China and the European Parliament reciprocally lifted restrictions on their exchanges, allowing legislative dialogue mechanisms to resume. He indicated that the National People's Congress is willing to strengthen contacts with the European Parliament and the national parliaments of the Member States of the European Union, with the aim of “increasing understanding, expanding consensus and promoting cooperation”, in order to boost stable and sustained development of relations between China and Europe. Lou Qinjian also stated that Iran's “sovereignty, security and territorial integrity” must be respected. The official also called for the immediate interruption of military actions, asking that a new escalation of tensions be avoided and that dialogue and negotiation be resumed to preserve peace and stability in the Middle East. Lou added that mutual respect and equality between large and small countries constitute “a requirement of historic progress” and a fundamental principle of the United Nations Charter. In this sense, he stressed that no State can act “at its pleasure on the international stage”. Lusa

item.title: Sam Hou Fai met with Xia Baolong in Beijing
item.description: Xia Baolong, director of the Hong Kong and Macau Affairs Office at the State Council, expressed his confidence in the Macau Government's implementation of the predominance of Executive power, as well as in increasing its governance effectiveness. The Chinese politician had a meeting yesterday with the Chief Executive of Macau, Sam Hou Fai, who is currently in Beijing to attend the opening session of the fourth session of the 14th National People's Congress (NPC), which will take place today. Segundo Xia Baolong, a sessão deste ano da APN vai analisar em breve o projecto do 15.º Plano Quinquenal Nacional e acredita-se que todos os sectores da sociedade dos governos das RAE vão “aproveitar esta oportunidade”. O responsável disse estar confiante de que o Governo de Macau irá formular bem o seu terceiro Plano Quinquenal com base nas circunstâncias reais da cidade. Xia Baolong espera ainda que Macau promova “ainda mais activamente” a diversificação da economia, em particular o desenvolvimento da Zona de Cooperação Aprofundada em Hengqin, para demonstrar, de forma contínua, o papel de Macau como “Um Centro, Uma Plataforma e Uma Base”, alcançandoassim um melhor desenvolvimento através da integração e do serviço ao panorama do desenvolvimento nacional. At the meeting, Xia Baolong praised Sam Hou Fai for his leadership of the MSAR government team in “resolutely implementing the guiding principles of President Xi Jinping's important speeches on Hong Kong and Macao affairs, alongside the policy decisions of the Central Government”. “Through bold reforms and vigorous efforts, the team firmly safeguarded national security, promoted reforms in public administration and actively participated in the development of the Greater Bay Area, achieving new progress in all sectors”, read a note released by the Hong Kong and Macau Affairs Office. It is recalled that Xia Baolong had already met on Tuesday with Macau members of the National People's Congress (NPC) and the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC). José Chui Sai Peng, APN deputy, recalled the meeting by stressing that Xia Baolong has “complete confidence” in Hong Kong and Macau, as they are in a “favorable moment” to develop with the “significant opportunities granted” by the Central Government and their “position supported by the country and linked to the world”.

item.title: China's industrial activity contracts for the second consecutive month
item.description:
China's industrial activity contracted again in February for the second consecutive month, according to official data released yesterday, although analysts believe that the recent reduction in North American tariffs could give a boost to the sector in the coming weeks.
The official manufacturing purchasing managers' index (PMI) fell to 49 points in February from 49.3 in January, reaching the lowest level in four months, China's National Bureau of Statistics said. The indicator, based on a monthly survey of industrial managers and calculated on a scale of 0 to 100, signals a contraction in activity when it falls below 50 points. In December, the industrial PMI had registered 50.1 points, interrupting eight consecutive months of contraction, but the return to negative territory suggests new fragility in the manufacturing industry, pressured by weak domestic demand. In a statement, National Bureau of Statistics statistician Huo Lihui attributed the weakening of data mainly to seasonal factors, including the Lunar New Year holiday, which this year lasted for nine days in mid-February. In contrast, a separate private sector survey also released yesterday presented a more positive picture. The PMI index from Chinese credit analysis company RatingDog rose to 52.1 points in February, compared to 50.3 in the previous month, remaining in expansion territory and recording the strongest growth since December 2020. This private indicator tends to better reflect the situation of small and medium-sized private companies, many of which are more export-oriented. According to Yao Yu, founder of RatingDog, foreign demand recovered in February and remained strong, with a significant increase in new export orders. Still, analysts consider that the data reveals an uneven recovery. “The mixed set of manufacturing PMI indicators suggests a similar trajectory to that seen in 2025,” said Lynn Song, chief economist for Greater China at ING bank. “Resilient external demand continues to drive growth, while domestic demand has been disappointingly weak,” he added. The decision by the United States Supreme Court last month, which annulled part of the reciprocal tariffs imposed by US President Donald Trump, thus reducing the rates applied globally – including on Chinese products – could also provide a “small boost” to exports and industrial activity in the coming months, according to Zichun Huang, economist at consultancy Capital Economics. Analysts also indicate that the meeting scheduled for April between Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping could pave the way for a longer trade truce between the world's two largest economies, which would be positive for Chinese manufacturers. The weakness of domestic demand is expected to continue to represent a challenge for the Chinese economy, in a context in which the prolonged crisis in the real estate sector has affected consumption and investment. This week, China is expected to announce the official economic growth target for this year during the annual meeting of the National People's Congress, which begins on Thursday. Economists expect Beijing to set a growth target of around 4.5% or higher. The meeting, which lasts for around a week, is also expected to approve the five-year strategic plan for 2026-2030, with an expected focus on strengthening technological innovation, industrial self-sufficiency and the development of strategic sectors. Lusa

item.title: Central Bank of Timor-Leste warns of circulation of counterfeit 200 cent coins
item.description: The Central Bank of Timor-Leste (BCTL) warned yesterday about the circulation in the national territory of counterfeit 200 cents (two dollars) coins from 2017 and asked the population to be alert. “We want to inform the public that, at this moment, BCTL is working together with the competent authorities”, said the person responsible for BCTL's monetary operations, António Espírito Santo, at a press conference in Dili. António Espírito Santo explained that the fake coins were identified on Friday, when commercial banks operating in the country took coins to BCTL for exchange. "The counterfeit currency produced by these criminals tries to imitate the original, but it is not the same. Technically, there are several differences" that one needs to be aware of, said António Espírito Santo. The original 200 cent coin is of good quality, heavy and in addition to the inscription of the year 2017, it has the BCTL mark in small letters, the design of Mount Matebian with a buffalo in the field, as well as the inscription “República Democrática de Timor-Leste”, with an accent on the “Ú” and “Á”, explained the person in charge. The assistant superintendent chief of operations at the Criminal Investigation Service Directorate, Mouzinho Correia, stated that the authorities are looking for the perpetrators of the crime and that the investigation process is ongoing. "In the case of these counterfeit coins, the State is the main victim, as is the community. Therefore, our agents on the ground are looking for those responsible", declared the investigating police commander. Counterfeit coins, according to those authorities, may be circulating in markets, stores, service stations, street vendors and other places where goods are bought and sold. The BCTL and the National Police of Timor-Leste (PNTL) call on the population throughout the national territory to carefully check currencies whenever carrying out transactions. "If, during a transaction, you suspect a counterfeit 200 cent coin, do not return it to circulation. You must immediately report it to the central bank or security authorities", appealed the monetary operations manager. António Espírito Santo also asked citizens, if they see or suspect individuals in possession of large quantities of counterfeit 200 cent coins, to immediately inform the competent authorities, such as the PNTL or the Scientific Criminal Investigation Police (PSIC). “When criminals are identified, the Central Bank and security authorities will take appropriate measures, in accordance with the powers assigned by law”, concluded António Espírito Santo.

item.title: Fake marriages have resulted in 46 BIR cancellations since 2016
item.description:
A total of 46 people had their Macau Identity Card canceled in the last decade due to their involvement in fake marriage cases. Sónia Chan, director of Identification Services, revealed that 280 reports of fake marriages have been received since 1999 and, in recent years, 25 cases have been condemned by the court.
The authorities canceled the Macau Identity Cards of 46 individuals who acquired resident status through false marriage. The number for the last ten years was the result of 25 cases of conviction by the court for this crime. Sónia Chan, director of Identification Services (DSI), explained that even when minors are involved, regardless of whether they were born in Macau, their identity cards are also revoked. "The process of canceling the BIR is carried out in strict accordance with the law. In the case of minors, if they came to Macau to settle with their mother, they will return with their mother to the Mainland. The same applies if they came with their father, with their BIR also being cancelled. For those born in Macau, we will deal in the same way", said the person in charge on the Chinese channel of Radio Macau. Sónia Chan added that the Executive has received approximately 280 reports of fake marriages since the creation of Macao SAR in 1999, and complaints from residents continue to be one of the main channels for detecting cases of marriage simulation. The DSI has established a marriage data verification mechanism, according to Sónia Chan, and has maintained cooperation with the Public Security Police Corps (CPSP) and police authorities in mainland China, with a view to combating illegal activities related to fake marriages. Through the aforementioned mechanism, when Mainland spouses want to settle in Macau, DSI assists Mainland public security authorities in verifying the spouses' information. Next, if suspicious cases are identified during the verification process, they will be forwarded to the CPSP for investigation, and may then be transferred to the Public Prosecutor's Office for follow-up.
BENEFITS OBTAINED MUST BE RETURNED
The CPSP, in this matter, said that last year 23 people had their residence permits declared invalid due to their involvement in fake marriages, including eight minors. As for case detection, from last year until February this year, the CPSP discovered and investigated 58 cases of fake marriage and 11 cases of illegally obtaining a family visit visa, involving 139 individuals. Among the cases, the longest recorded duration of a fraudulent marriage was 19 years. Lao Ka Weng, head of the Border Control Department, indicated that after conviction, the individuals will also be prosecuted by the DSI for the benefits obtained during the period in which they had their right of residence in Macau. According to the spokesperson, in cases of false marriage, the average illicit profits amounted to 60,000 patacas, with the highest amount reaching 120,000 patacas. Eight intermediaries who facilitated fake marriages were arrested, having collected illicit amounts ranging from several thousand to 20,000 patacas. Lao Ka Weng pointed out that, in these cases, the modus operandi always involves family members organizing fake marriages with acquaintances, mainly to help family members improve their living conditions. “Several cases also involve criminals who specifically target single or divorced residents of Macau, exploiting their limited legal knowledge and luring them with false promises of financial gains or care in old age, to falsely ‘marry’”, he highlighted. With regard to the application for a Residence Permit based on family grouping, the CPSP received, from last year until February this year, more than 5,000 requests. Among these, more than 4,300 residents of mainland China submitted their application through the “One Way” safe conduct scheme for Hong Kong and Macau, which is commonly known as “single safe conduct”, followed by more than 300 Hong Kong identity card holders.

item.title: China, US hold high-level trade talks ahead of Trump visit
item.description: China and the United States are expected to hold a new round of high-level trade negotiations in Paris at the end of next week, as preparations continue for the US President's visit to Beijing. The delegations will be led by Chinese Vice Prime Minister He Lifeng and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who are expected to discuss trade and investment agreements – including tariffs, bilateral investment, soybeans and rare earth minerals – which could be presented as concrete results when US President Donald Trump meets with Chinese President Xi Jinping in the following weeks, according to sources cited by the Hong Kong newspaper South China Morning Post. The White House announced that Trump will travel to China between March 31 and April 2, in what will be his first visit to the country since he returned to the presidency in early 2025. Beijing has not yet officially confirmed the schedule, although it has stated that both parties “maintain communication at all levels”. According to the same sources, the meeting in Paris – whose exact date and location may still change – will be the sixth round of trade negotiations between Beijing and Washington since Trump began, in April last year, an unprecedented tariff war against Chinese products. Previous rounds were held in Geneva, London, Stockholm, Madrid and Kuala Lumpur, resulting in a significant reduction in mutual tariffs, which reached levels above 100% during the height of the commercial escalation. The tariff panorama between the world's two largest economies could enter a new phase of uncertainty following a historic decision by the United States Supreme Court in February, which annulled 20% tariffs imposed by Trump on Chinese products last year. The decision forced the US administration to look for legal alternatives to maintain commercial pressure on Beijing. Trade rivalry between the United States and China has intensified over the last decade, marked by disputes over trade imbalances, market access, industrial subsidies and dominance in strategic sectors. During Trump's first term (2017-2021), Washington imposed tariffs on hundreds of billions of dollars in Chinese products, prompting Beijing to respond with equivalent measures. Despite several partial agreements and periods of truce, tensions remained in the following years, especially in areas such as technology, supply chains and critical minerals.
